pr.title: Split out rest of character-specific items
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Finished splitting remaining character-specific item assembly out of the large item translation-unit assembly into per-item asm files under asm/melee/it/items. The PR moved Game & Watch and Young Link-related item code/data from asm/melee/it/it_27CF.s and asm/melee/it/it_803F5468.s into dedicated files, updated obj_files.mk to build those objects, and added clarifying comments for several ItemLogicTable entries in src/melee/it/it_803F3100.c. Evidence indicates this was primarily a mechanical organization/link-layout split rather than a behavioral rewrite.
what_changed: Removed about 2028 lines from asm/melee/it/it_27CF.s, extracting item code that had previously lived in the omnibus item assembly file.

Removed about 60 lines of item-specific data from asm/melee/it/it_803F5468.s, including symbols such as it_803F7958, it_803F7968, it_803F7978, it_803F79A0, it_803F79C0, it_803F79E0, it_803F7A00, and it_803F7A28.
Added six dedicated item asm files: itkirbygamewatchchefpan.s, itgamewatchjudge.s, itgamewatchpanic.s, itgamewatchrescue.s, itgamewatchchef.s, and itclinkmilk.s.
Moved relevant .text, .data, and .sdata2 content into the new per-item files while preserving original global labels and address-based symbol names such as it_802C8B28 and it_804DD2D0.
Updated obj_files.mk to include the six new item assembly object files in TEXT_O_FILES before the remaining it_803F5468.s.o and it_27CF.s.o entries.
Added comments to four entries in struct ItemLogicTable it_803F3100[118]: Game & Watch panic, Game & Watch chef, Young Link milk, and Game & Watch rescue.
smart_moves: The split was done as a mostly one-to-one relocation: line counts show large deletions from the old aggregate files and nearly equivalent additions to new files, reducing risk of behavior changes.
Keeping original global symbol names allowed existing call sites and item logic table references to remain valid without broad source churn.
Moving item-specific data and constants with the corresponding item asm files made each file closer to a self-contained future decompilation unit.
Updating obj_files.mk in the same PR avoided leaving newly split asm files unlinked or relying on stale omnibus object coverage.
Adding ItemLogicTable comments created lightweight semantic anchors for otherwise address-named data entries.
decomp_lessons: When preparing item code for future C decompilation, first isolate character/item-specific assembly into dedicated files while preserving labels and link order.
For mechanical asm splits, move associated .data and .sdata2 symbols alongside .text so constants, assert strings, and action-state data stay discoverable with the item implementation.
Small comments in central item tables are useful when symbols are still mostly address-based; they document which item a table entry represents without forcing premature renames.
Build-file ordering is part of the matching surface for asm split PRs; new object entries should be inserted where the old code lived rather than appended arbitrarily.
A PR with no review discussion and no explanatory body should be treated as evidence of a low-risk structural move only; do not infer semantic fixes beyond the diff.
naming_conventions: Per-item asm filenames use lowercase item names under asm/melee/it/items, e.g. itgamewatchchef.s, itgamewatchjudge.s, itgamewatchpanic.s, itgamewatchrescue.s, itkirbygamewatchchefpan.s, and itclinkmilk.s.
The file name itclinkmilk.s likely follows the project item naming convention for character-prefixed item files, but the table comment labels the item as Young Link milk.
Address-based function and data symbols were preserved, e.g. it_802C8B28, it_802C76E8, it_803F79E0, and it_804DD2D0.
Human-readable item identity was added as comments in ItemLogicTable entries rather than by renaming unresolved symbols.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split a monolithic asm file into per-item files without rewriting instructions.
Preserved .include "macros.inc", .section .text, .section .data, .section .sdata2, .balign, and .global directives in the new assembly files.
Moved data tables and assert strings out of asm/melee/it/it_803F5468.s to keep item-local data near the item code that references it.
Maintained existing labels for branch targets, globals, constants, and callback tables so relocation/reference matching remained stable.
Inserted new object files in obj_files.mk adjacent to existing Game & Watch item assembly objects and before residual aggregate item objects.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
searchable_terms: Split out rest of character-specific items
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile itgamewatchchef.s or itgamewatchjudge.s from asm to C.
Search for ItemLogicTable comments added around it_803F79A0, it_803F79C0, it_803F79E0, and it_803F7A28 to map item IDs.
Inspect subsequent changes to obj_files.mk around Game & Watch item object ordering for matching-sensitive build lessons.
Look for later renames of itclinkmilk or Young Link milk functions to understand final naming conventions.
